canonical_definition: The process of reducing the length of a prompt, typically by summarizing or extracting key information from lengthy context documents, to make it more efficient and focused for a language model.
papers: Robust and Unbounded Length Generalization in Autoregressive Transformer-Based Text-to-Speech
Self-Pluralising Culture Alignment for Large Language Models

relationships.paper: Yeah, Un, Oh: Continuous and Real-time Backchannel Prediction with Fine-tuning of Voice Activity Projection
relationships.evidence: “In agent, API documentation can be compressed to enable more efficient tool use”
